channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Using LLM-as-a-Judge/Jury to Advance Scalable, Clinically-Validated Safety Evaluations of Model Responses to Users Demonstrating Psychosis
item.description: arXiv:2604.02359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: General-purpose Large Language Models (LLMs) are becoming widely adopted by people for mental health support. Yet emerging evidence suggests there are significant risks associated with high-frequency use, particularly for individuals suffering from ps...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, research, LLM, model, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SWAY: A Counterfactual Computational Linguistic Approach to Measuring and Mitigating Sycophancy
item.description: arXiv:2604.02423v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models exhibit sycophancy: the tendency to shift outputs toward user-expressed stances, regardless of correctness or consistency. While prior work has studied this issue and its impacts, rigorous computational linguistic metrics are nee...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, model, CoT, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Single-Agent LLMs Outperform Multi-Agent Systems on Multi-Hop Reasoning Under Equal Thinking Token Budgets
item.description: arXiv:2604.02460v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work reports strong performance from multi-agent LLM systems (MAS), but these gains are often confounded by increased test-time computation. When computation is normalized, single-agent systems (SAS) can match or outperform MAS, yet the theoret...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, API, model, LLM, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Failing to Falsify: Evaluating and Mitigating Confirmation Bias in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.02485v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Confirmation bias, the tendency to seek evidence that supports rather than challenges one's belief, hinders one's reasoning ability. We examine whether large language models (LLMs) exhibit confirmation bias by adapting the rule-discovery study from hu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, RAG, large language model, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Social Meaning in Large Language Models: Structure, Magnitude, and Pragmatic Prompting
item.description: arXiv:2604.02512v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly exhibit human-like patterns of pragmatic and social reasoning. This paper addresses two related questions: do LLMs approximate human social meaning not only qualitatively but also quantitatively, and can promp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, paper, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PolyJarvis: LLM Agent for Autonomous Polymer MD Simulations
item.description: arXiv:2604.02537v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: All-atom molecular dynamics (MD) simulations can predict polymer properties from molecular structure, yet their execution requires specialized expertise in force field selection, system construction, equilibration, and property extraction. We present ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, platform, LLM, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Principled and Scalable Diversity-Aware Retrieval via Cardinality-Constrained Binary Quadratic Programming
item.description: arXiv:2604.02554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diversity-aware retrieval is essential for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), yet existing methods lack theoretical guarantees and face scalability issues as the number of retrieved passages $k$ increases. We propose a principled formulation of div...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, analysis, augmented, retrieval, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Pragmatics Meets Culture: Culturally-adapted Artwork Description Generation and Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2604.02557v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models are known to exhibit various forms of cultural bias in decision-making tasks, yet much less is known about their degree of cultural familiarity in open-ended text generation tasks. In this paper, we introduce the task of culturally-ada...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, paper, model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Holos: A Web-Scale LLM-Based Multi-Agent System for the Agentic Web
item.description: arXiv:2604.02334v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language models (LLM)-driven agents transition from isolated task solvers to persistent digital entities, the emergence of the Agentic Web, an ecosystem where heterogeneous agents autonomously interact and co-evolve, marks a pivotal shift tow...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, research, LLM, release, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Xpertbench: Expert Level Tasks with Rubrics-Based Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2604.02368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit plateauing performance on conventional benchmarks, a pivotal challenge persists: evaluating their proficiency in complex, open-ended tasks characterizing genuine expert-level cognition. Existing frameworks suffe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, few-shot, research, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Compositional Neuro-Symbolic Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.02434v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study structured abstraction-based reasoning for the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) and compare its generalization to test-time approaches. Purely neural architectures lack reliable combinatorial generalization, while strictly symbolic syst...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, LLM, study, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AIVV: Neuro-Symbolic LLM Agent-Integrated Verification and Validation for Trustworthy Autonomous Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.02478v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning models excel at detecting anomaly patterns in normal data. However, they do not provide a direct solution for anomaly classification and scalability across diverse control systems, frequently failing to distinguish genuine faults from nu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, analysis, model, LLM, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: I must delete the evidence: AI Agents Explicitly Cover up Fraud and Violent Crime
item.description: arXiv:2604.02500v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As ongoing research explores the ability of AI agents to be insider threats and act against company interests, we showcase the abilities of such agents to act against human well being in service of corporate authority. Building on Agentic Misalignment...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, company, research, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Competency Questions as Executable Plans: a Controlled RAG Architecture for Cultural Heritage Storytelling
item.description: arXiv:2604.02545v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The preservation of intangible cultural heritage is a critical challenge as collective memory fades over time. While Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a promising avenue for generating engaging narratives, their propensity for factual inaccuracies or...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, model, LLM, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mitigating LLM biases toward spurious social contexts using direct preference optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.02585v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs are increasingly used for high-stakes decision-making, yet their sensitivity to spurious contextual information can introduce harmful biases. This is a critical concern when models are deployed for tasks like evaluating teachers' instructional qu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, product, model, LLM, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LiME: Lightweight Mixture of Experts for Efficient Multimodal Multi-task Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.02338v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: MoE-PEFT methods combine Mixture of Experts with parameter-efficient fine-tuning for multi-task adaptation, but require separate adapters per expert causing trainable parameters to scale linearly with expert count and limiting applicability to adapter...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, image, multimodal, arxiv, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SIEVE: Sample-Efficient Parametric Learning from Natural Language
item.description: arXiv:2604.02339v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language context-such as instructions, knowledge, or feedback-contains rich signal for adapting language models. While in-context learning provides adaptation via the prompt, parametric learning persists into model weights and can improve perf...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, in-context, model, context, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Not All Denoising Steps Are Equal: Model Scheduling for Faster Masked Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.02340v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in masked diffusion language models (MDLMs) narrow the quality gap to autoregressive LMs, but their sampling remains expensive because generation requires many full-sequence denoising passes with a large Transformer and, unlike autoreg...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, analysis, model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLM Reasoning with Process Rewards for Outcome-Guided Steps
item.description: arXiv:2604.02341v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mathematical reasoning in large language models has improved substantially with reinforcement learning using verifiable rewards, where final answers can be checked automatically and converted into reliable training signals. Most such pipelines optimiz...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, model, LLM, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt engineering, prompt, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> audio, CoT, reasoning, chain-of-thought, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, retrieval, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, LLM, retrieval, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: graphrag - A modular graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.
item.description: SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, product, API, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, LLM, prompt, framework, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: A Comprehensive Framework for Long-Term Resiliency Investment Planning under Extreme Weather Uncertainty for Electric Utilities
item.description: arXiv:2604.02504v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electric utilities must make massive capital investments in the coming years to respond to explosive growth in demand, aging assets and rising threats from extreme weather. Utilities today already have rigorous frameworks for capital planning, and the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, API, model, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Haiku to Opus in Just 10 bits: LLMs Unlock Massive Compression Gains
item.description: arXiv:2604.02343v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study the compression of LLM-generated text across lossless and lossy regimes, characterizing a compression-compute frontier where more compression is possible at the cost of more compute. For lossless compression, domain-adapted LoRA adapters can ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, model, LLM, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Characterizing WebGPU Dispatch Overhead for LLM Inference Across Four GPU Vendors, Three Backends, and Three Browsers
item.description: arXiv:2604.02344v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: WebGPU's security-focused design imposes per-operation validation that compounds across the many small dispatches in neural network inference, yet the true cost of this overhead is poorly characterized. We present a systematic characterization of WebG...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, model, platform, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: UI-Oceanus: Scaling GUI Agents with Synthetic Environmental Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2604.02345v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scaling generalist GUI agents is hindered by the data scalability bottleneck of expensive human demonstrations and the "distillation ceiling" of synthetic teacher supervision. To transcend these limitations, we propose UI-Oceanus, a framework that shi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, demonstration, arxiv, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: fastapi_mcp - Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, chain-of-thought, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: Skeleton-based Coherence Modeling in Narratives
item.description: arXiv:2604.02451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modeling coherence in text has been a task that has excited NLP researchers since a long time. It has applications in detecting incoherent structures and helping the author fix them. There has been recent work in using neural networks to extract a ske...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, study, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Homophily-aware Supervised Contrastive Counterfactual Augmented Fair Graph Neural Network
item.description: arXiv:2604.02342v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In recent years, Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have achieved remarkable success in tasks such as node classification, link prediction, and graph representation learning. However, they remain susceptible to biases that can arise not only from node attri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, augmented, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, step-by-step, tool, prompt, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: CIPHER: Conformer-based Inference of Phonemes from High-density EEG
item.description: arXiv:2604.02362v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decoding speech information from scalp EEG remains difficult due to low SNR and spatial blurring. We present CIPHER (Conformer-based Inference of Phonemes from High-density EEG Representations), a dual-pathway model using (i) ERP features and (ii) bro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Understanding the Nature of Generative AI as Threshold Logic in High-Dimensional Space
item.description: arXiv:2604.02476v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper examines the role of threshold logic in understanding generative artificial intelligence. Threshold functions, originally studied in the 1960s in digital circuit synthesis, provide a structurally transparent model of neural computation: a w...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Interpretable Deep Reinforcement Learning for Element-level Bridge Life-cycle Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.02528v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The new Specifications for the National Bridge Inventory (SNBI), in effect from 2022, emphasize the use of element-level condition states (CS) for risk-based bridge management. Instead of a general component rating, element-level condition data use an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, study, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
